steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts two theories of bipedalism’s origin: ground-based knuckle-walking and tree-based observations in orangutans. The decisive signal is the contrast word "But" introducing the orangutan example as an alternative to the ground-based theory.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice A suggests that bipedalism could have evolved advantageous for tree-dwelling ancestors, directly aligning with the orangutan evidence as an alternative to the ground-based theory. The other three either reverse the relationship (B, C) or conflate knuckle-walking with tree movement (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
